name: ServiceNow, Inc.

profile.description: ServiceNow, Inc. provides cloud-based digital workflow solutions for enterprise IT, employee, and customer workflows. The Now Platform serves as an 'AI agent control tower' for enterprise operations, with products spanning IT service management, IT operations management, customer service, HR delivery, and security operations. Founded in 2004, headquartered in Santa Clara, California, with a market capitalization of approximately $105 billion.

innovation.label: STRONG
innovation.analysis: Track Record (3/3): ServiceNow has executed a remarkable transformation from an IT ticketing tool to the enterprise's 'AI agent control tower,' growing subscription revenues from $3B (2019) to $12.9B (2025) — a complete platform reinvention. The acquisition of Moveworks (AI company) and positioning as an agentic AI orchestration layer represent transformative business model evolution [1]. AI Deployment (2/2): ServiceNow is shipping AI-powered products across its entire platform — agentic AI for IT operations, customer service, and HR workflows. Clients include FedEx, AstraZeneca, NHL, and Ulta Beauty with measurable AI-driven automation. CEO McDermott states AI enables the company to avoid backfilling attrition, demonstrating internal AI cost savings [2]. Market Share (2/2): ServiceNow dominates IT service management and is the fastest-growing enterprise workflow platform, with CEO McDermott claiming 'AI and ServiceNow are devouring many SaaS vendors' as the platform consolidates point solutions [2]. 22% subscription revenue growth in a maturing enterprise software market demonstrates accelerating share gains. Product Pipeline (1/2): Moveworks acquisition adds 100bps to subscription growth; agentic AI capabilities and the 'AI agent control tower' positioning are announced broadly but specific next-gen product timelines with revenue visibility are less concrete [1]. R&D Intensity (1/1): ServiceNow's technology investment is substantial — the Moveworks acquisition, internal AI development, and platform R&D spending are well above industry average for enterprise SaaS [1].

inspiration.label: DEVELOPING
inspiration.analysis: Mission Floor (2/4): ServiceNow's purpose is to 'make the world work better for everyone' — clear, service-oriented, and connects the company's workflow automation to universal productivity gains, but it's broad enough to apply to many technology companies and doesn't articulate a distinctive transformative vision [2]. Community Impact (1/2): ServiceNow promotes ESG management solutions and has positioned itself as a leader in corporate responsibility technology, though specific charitable dollar amounts or distinctive community programs were not identified beyond standard corporate initiatives [3]. Employee Retention (2/2): ServiceNow employs 29,187 people and head of global innovation Brian Solis has emphasized that 'innovation is no longer just about technology, it's about rethinking value itself,' reflecting a mission-driven culture. CEO McDermott stated the company would maintain headcount by not backfilling attrition (using AI), while emphasizing 'we can still have a great culture' — suggesting employee retention is a priority [2]. Insider Buying (1/2): CEO McDermott's compensation is heavily equity-based and he has maintained significant ServiceNow holdings, signaling long-term conviction, though no specific net insider buying was identified in the last 12 months [1].

infrastructure.label: STRONG
infrastructure.analysis: Mechanically scored on available metrics. ServiceNow delivered FY2025 subscription revenues of $12.88B (up 21%) with Q1 2026 subscription revenues of $3.67B (up 22%), and FY2026 guidance of $15.5-15.6B [1]. D/E of 20.7% is well below the 70% target (score 1.0). ROE of 16.1% exceeds the 15% target (score 1.0 at 2x weight). Revenue growth YoY of 22.1% exceeds the 10% target (score 1.0). 5yr revenue CAGR of 22.4% exceeds the 10% target (score 1.0 at 2x weight). Forward P/E of 20.3 is essentially at the 20 target (score 0.99). Profit margin of 12.6% is below the 20% target but above the 0% floor (score 0.63). Forward PEG of 0.99 is at the 1.0 target (score 1.0 at 2x weight). Liquidity: $27.1M avg volume x $102.15 = $2.77B — passes easily [4].

ai_resilience.label: MODERATE
ai_resilience.analysis: AI Risk Protection (5/9): ServiceNow's enterprise workflow platform faces a nuanced AI disruption risk. CEO McDermott argues that 'enterprise operations are too interconnected for point solutions to handle,' positioning ServiceNow as essential infrastructure that makes AI useful rather than a victim of AI substitution [2]. However, the company operates in the EXPOSED zone because: (1) large language models could automate IT service management workflows that constitute ServiceNow's core, (2) competitors like Salesforce are aggressively deploying agentic AI in overlapping domains, and (3) hyperscalers (Microsoft, Google) could build competing workflow automation into their platforms. Analysts note the battle between McDermott and Salesforce's Benioff over agentic AI dominance creates real competitive uncertainty [2]. AI Opportunity (1/1): ServiceNow generates substantial AI-related revenue through its AI-powered workflow products, agentic AI capabilities, and the Moveworks acquisition. The company's 22% subscription growth is explicitly driven by AI demand, and specific clients (FedEx, AstraZeneca) are deploying ServiceNow's AI products at scale [1][2].

infinite_game.summary: ServiceNow under Bill McDermott is playing a moderately infinite game, driven by a genuine belief that enterprise workflow automation can transform how organizations operate. McDermott's leadership combines the charisma and long-term thinking of an infinite player with the competitive intensity of a finite one — his framing of 'devouring' SaaS competitors reflects a win-oriented mindset even as the underlying platform strategy serves a cause bigger than quarterly results.

just_cause.analysis: Just Cause (2/3): 'Making the world work better for everyone' is a genuine cause that connects ServiceNow's workflow platform to universal productivity improvement. Strategic decisions — investing billions in AI, acquiring Moveworks, building agentic AI capabilities — trace back to this purpose [2]. The cause has been consistent through leadership transitions (from Fred Luddy to John Donahoe to Bill McDermott). However, it hasn't been tested through a true crisis that would validate whether leadership would sacrifice short-term results to preserve the cause.

courage_to_lead.analysis: Courage to Lead (2/2): McDermott has demonstrated a pattern of courageous long-term bets: acquiring Moveworks to accelerate AI capabilities despite integration risk, publicly stating the company won't backfill attrition (betting on AI productivity over headcount), and positioning ServiceNow as the 'AI agent control tower' — a bold claim that invites competitive retaliation from Microsoft, Salesforce, and hyperscalers [2]. His willingness to openly challenge Silicon Valley's approach to enterprise AI ('Silicon Valley is getting enterprise AI wrong') reflects conviction over consensus-seeking [2].

trusting_teams.analysis: Trusting Teams (1/2): ServiceNow employs 29,187 people and head of global innovation Brian Solis has articulated a vision of innovation as 'rethinking value itself,' suggesting an empowered culture [2]. McDermott's statement about maintaining headcount while using AI for productivity — 'we can still have a great culture' — signals awareness that trust matters. However, the not-backfilling-attrition policy could undermine trust among employees who see AI as a threat to their colleagues' jobs.

existential_flexibility.analysis: Existential Flexibility (1/2): ServiceNow has meaningfully evolved from an IT ticketing tool to an enterprise-wide workflow automation platform, entering customer service, HR, security operations, and now agentic AI orchestration. Each expansion was driven by the cause of making work better, not desperation [1]. However, the core platform architecture has remained consistent — this is expansion rather than the kind of radical pivot Sinek describes (like Apple abandoning the Mac for iPhone).

worthy_rivals.analysis: Worthy Rivals (0/1): McDermott's competitive language is distinctly finite — 'AI and ServiceNow are devouring many SaaS vendors,' the public battle with Salesforce's Benioff over agentic AI dominance, and the framing of 'winning' against point solution vendors [2]. This is competition to destroy, not competition to learn. While the competitive intensity drives innovation, Sinek would identify this as a finite competitive mindset.

investment_thesis: ServiceNow delivered FY2025 subscription revenues of $12.88B (up 21%) with remarkable consistency across all four quarters, and Q1 2026 accelerated to 22% YoY growth ($3.67B) with FY2026 guidance of $15.5-15.6B [1]. The company is the premier beneficiary of enterprise AI adoption — its 'AI agent control tower' positioning makes it the orchestration layer for agentic AI across IT, customer service, HR, and security operations. With Forward PEG of 0.99, D/E of 20.7%, and $5.1B in free cash flow, ServiceNow offers rare combination of 20%+ growth at reasonable valuation with a fortress balance sheet. The Moveworks acquisition adds 100bps to growth and deepens AI capabilities. CEO McDermott's vision of 'devouring' SaaS point solutions through platform consolidation has a long runway as enterprises simplify their tech stacks [2].
thesis_continued: The primary risk is that ServiceNow's premium valuation (60.8x trailing P/E) requires sustained 20%+ growth that becomes mathematically harder as the revenue base approaches $16B. Profit margin of 12.6% is modest for enterprise SaaS and reflects heavy investment in AI and the Moveworks integration — margin expansion must materialize to justify the multiple [1]. The AI disruption risk is real: Microsoft Copilot, Salesforce Agentforce, and hyperscaler workflow tools could commoditize IT service management, and CEO Benioff has publicly targeted ServiceNow's market position [2]. Earnings growth of just 2.3% YoY in the most recent period, despite 22% revenue growth, highlights the margin pressure from AI investment. The company has no dividend and does not return capital through buybacks, meaning shareholders depend entirely on price appreciation. The Inspire Impact Score of -49 (MISALIGNED) may deter faith-based investors.
key_catalysts: FY2026 subscription revenue guidance of $15.5-15.6B representing 21% growth, with Moveworks contributing 100bps and agentic AI demand accelerating enterprise adoption
Enterprise AI platform consolidation driving ServiceNow's position as the 'AI agent control tower,' displacing point-solution SaaS vendors and expanding total addressable market
Margin expansion from AI-driven productivity gains — McDermott's plan to maintain headcount by not backfilling attrition could improve operating leverage as revenue scales
key_risks: Microsoft Copilot, Salesforce Agentforce, and hyperscaler workflow tools could commoditize IT service management and workflow automation — ServiceNow's core revenue base faces direct competitive pressure from larger, better-capitalized platforms
Trailing P/E of 60.8x requires sustained 20%+ growth that becomes mathematically harder as revenue approaches $16B — any deceleration would trigger significant multiple compression

Profit margin of 12.6% is modest for enterprise SaaS, and earnings growth of 2.3% (versus 22% revenue growth) reveals margin pressure from AI investment and the Moveworks integration that must resolve for the thesis to hold
sources: ServiceNow Q4 2025 and Q1 2026 Earnings Reports and FY2026 Guidance — sec.gov and CNBC, January and April 2026
ServiceNow CEO Bill McDermott on AI Strategy, Leadership, and Competition — CNBC, Fast Company, Cloud Wars, TechBuzz, 2025-2026
ServiceNow ESG Products and Corporate Sustainability — servicenow.com, accessed June 2026
Yahoo Finance — NOW Key Statistics and Financial Data, accessed June 2026
